expectations.text: Includes hero section with headline, subheadline, and CTA
expectations.evidence: All 3 variations have hero section with headline, subheadline, and CTA. Example: 'Your ROAS Looks Great. Your Margins Tell a Different Story.' + subheadline + 'See Your Profit Map — Book a 15-Minute Demo'.

expectations.text: Has two distinct content blocks below the hero
expectations.evidence: Each variation has 2 content blocks with clear section headings: V1 ('Revenue Attribution Is Lying to You' + 'One Dashboard. Every Channel. Actual Profit.'), V2 ('ROAS Is a Revenue Metric...' + 'Built for the Stack You Already Use'), V3 ('You Have the Data...' + 'What Changes When You See Profit...').

expectations.text: Headline leads with benefit/outcome, not just the product name
expectations.evidence: All 3 headlines lead with benefit: 'Your ROAS Looks Great. Your Margins Tell a Different Story.' / 'Finally Know Which Marketing Channels Actually Make You Money' / 'Stop Scaling the Campaigns That Are Losing You Money'. MetricShift name appears only in subheadlines.

expectations.text: Includes social proof elements or placeholders
expectations.evidence: Notes section explicitly recommends adding customer logos, named CMO testimonials, and specific metrics. V2 includes marketing team reference. Detailed guidance on where and how to add social proof.

expectations.text: Uses specific data or numbers rather than vague claims
expectations.evidence: Multiple specific claims: 'under 10 minutes' setup, 'profit contribution per channel, per campaign, per SKU', '5x ROAS' example, '30% return rates' scenario. Concrete examples of what the dashboard shows.

expectations.text: Authoritative B2B tone without corporate jargon
expectations.evidence: Confident, peer-to-peer B2B tone. Uses industry terms (ROAS, COGS, contribution margin) that the CMO audience understands, without generic buzzwords. No 'leverage', 'synergy', or 'best-in-class'.
